item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There really isn't much of anything here other than a somewhat weird video game cabinet. It doesn't really do much of anything. The imagery is cool, but … eh. It's nowhere near enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even though it's similar to Polybius, it does carry a sort of charm. Being haunted by video games seems interesting to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's …… too similar a reference to Polybius.-1

Fixed that for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's …… too similar to Polybius.-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a… Polybius. Just a glitchy ol' "haunted" arcade game that drives you crazy and kills you. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ahh alright. That makes sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At least it doesn't spray blood in your face and tried being pretentious and in your face about how "sonic is a nightmarish, demented, all-powerful monster."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Polybius and being challenging thing seems like a step in the right direction, but the nightmares and deaths just feel too shoehorned in. I'd like this more if it was just an arcade cabinet that adapts itself to make you addicted to it and give it more money.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sonic.exe ripoff. *Yawn*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, but "the game itself" could've had more going on.

Also, if I'm being honest, the stuff you mentioned really isn't enough to support an article at this point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not badly written, but the whole "it's a glitch, then you die" part reads like a creepypasta.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's very little meat here aside from references, both to Polybius and to classic arcade games. Take those away, it's a spooky box that makes people go crazy. I'll pass.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nothing eerie? You get hallucinations and creepy glitches while playing it! How is that not eerie?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It needs more of a hook, I guess. The Polybius connection is cool but there's nothing really upsetting or eerie about the game itself, and I feel like there's a lot of opportunity to insert something more unsettling. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's Polybius. Really liked the empty spaceship stage. Love it +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find this somewhat similar to the urban legend "Polybius".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upon a re-read, I found this to be rather unsettling in a good way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to agree with the majority here. There does not seem to be any obvious hook here, 30% of players having nightmares is rather tame for what has come to be expected here. I might see some room for expansion if it had another anomalous property, like there were more than just one and they showed up at random in arcades with no history of being ordered … but as is this SCP falls flat for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Anyone else having a problem viewing the image? It loads, then vanishes. Should I just assume wikidot is being stupid again?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When I think of something that improves the "anomalousness" of the article without detracting from it, you guys will be the first to know.

Also, who said this makes people go crazy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm finding myself agreeing with the people on the first page, I just don't really see this as an SCP. it causes you to die because you went crazy because you played a video game? Also, the information at the end seems kind of tacked on to me, like it was just added in order for this to be somewhat anomalous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a great example of a simple concept, efficiently written, with the right amount of interesting, intriguing details to work. Very nicely done.

Side note, opinion #3 on the [DATA EXPUNGED]: I found it completely inoffensive, as it neither bothered me nor struck me as a gold standard example.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, Sweet Fancy Moses!

I do rather like your game, sir. Excellently done, and I thank you for contributing it to the world.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That I am.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an awesome first thing to read after my vacation

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait… Did you… Are you the guy who made Eversion?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good man.

I love your books btw.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Image by Guilherme S. Tows (myself), Attribution-ShareAlike 3.0 license (same as the page it's on)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I can't see how this counts at all either. It loaded on another console which would solidly qualify it for SCP status, but they have no verification of that and beyond that there's nothing more to this than a creepy game with lots of bugs.

That, and the one solidly anomalous thing about it (the deaths) seems tacked on and unrelated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm torn between liking this for being subtle and disliking it for not being sufficiently anomalous. Heck with it, I'll upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As long as it goes no farther than that — it doesn't directly drive you insane — I really like that idea.

Stay clear of quarters, kids.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: THANK YOU, I thought that was actually quite the clever use of it. Yeah I know, it could be more explicit, but think about it: what could be such a thing, that even an already contained video game featuring crude sprites of it would have to be expunged? THAT, is horror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
OH NO ITS AN ARCADE GAME IT'S OBVIOUSLY POLYBIUS

Seriously though upvote, this is simple and clever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nyancat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know how "cellar door" is supposed to be the most beautiful phrase in written English?

The "Jungle" stage featuring [DATA EXPUNGED] as enemies.

I don't know why, but this is literally one of the best examples of expungement I've ever seen. If I were to write a guide just about expungements, this would be the Gold Standard. I can't explain it—it just does exactly what it's supposed to do, no more, no less.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting. I like your graphics.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
yeah, again people get nightmares about things they do, i have nightmares about my school, that doesn't make it an SCP. and as far as going crazy, that happens all the time to, there are people in REAL LIFE who think they are vampires, that does not make Twilight, Underworld or any other poorly written media and SCP

TL;DR : if nighmares and going insane made something an SCP, pretty much everything is an SCP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Polybius

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Polybius?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or perhaps when someone gets on Hi-score list, that person dies short time after?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "quarters" should probably be "United States quarters"-at least in the first instance- for clarity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I drew it myself. And apparently did a good job of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not Galaga. I'd lay odds to it being Terra Cresta.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What about this?

Every time you make some transaction involving quarters, (after having played the game) you hear the game launching. Subsequently, you start hearing sounds indicating that you've lost a life, or cleared a stage. Listening carefully, you might find that other sounds from the game underlay real ones. The more quarters spent since playing, the easier it gets to make these sounds out, and the harder it gets to make out real ones. All game sounds end upon a game over (unless you got a continue with another transaction). Real sounds remain difficult to make out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm… I do think that the anomalous properties need to be somewhat more explicit. As it is, it could just be that those kids dying is completely coincidental to their intials appears on the high score. Perhaps if there was a note that a D-class tester died of natural causes before the 30-day window & his/her intials then appeared in the high score list. I like the effort put into this object; it's just that right now it's a glitchy game and that's it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What game is that in the picture? is it Galaxian or Galaga?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The anomalous effects do seem tacked on at the end. They are also not presented in a way to make me care much about them. The major effect is that some people have nightmares.
I think it could become a solid entry if the creepy factor was reworked and improved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: POLYBIUS IS.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: About 30% of SCP-1881-B players report lasting psychological effects afterwards, in the form of nightmares related to its contents and occasional visual/auditory hallucinations (a common complaint is hearing sounds from the game when unaccompained and in quiet areas). One interviewed case, who had access to SCP-1881 before it was contained, reported his home videogame console would occasionally launch SCP-1881-B instead of whatever game was installed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree. I like this one so far but I think it needs a better topping than the standard mysterideath ending to flesh it out. Otherwise, I'm excited!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: not voting for now because i am clearly missing something. i honestly see nothing remotely anomalous about this. its an arcade game that has strange levels, and there are a string of deaths involving it. if anything its an anomalous item, can someone explain this to me?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
AND THEN CRAZY TO DEATH!

Of which I thoroughly was rubbed the wrong way and downvoted.

Also:

The "Jungle" stage featuring [DATA EXPUNGED] as enemies.

Irks me to no end, this one. I bet it was ponies. Rainbow ponies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd tend to agree, I was thoroughly enjoying it until 'makes you crazy then dead' kicked in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, seeing as the internal mechanisms are [DATA EXPUNGED], who's to say it even has a Gamecart?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I may be missing something but the deaths in the addendum seem a bit shoehorned in…?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A good Arcade SCP. I like.
I am curious though; Is it the Arcade Cabinet(screen included) or the Gamecart that would be the actual SCP? Or would both be needed together to produce said effects?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well done

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is actually rather clever. I like it.
